source: runtime MCP tools/list from dist/index.js over in-memory MCP transport

tools.name: approval_list
tools.description: List recent MCP-managed approval decisions recorded by the gateway (approvalStrategy: mcp_managed).

cli.description: Optional CLI filter (any gateway CLI provider, derived from CLI_TYPES)

tools.name: ask_model
tools.description: Ask one provider CLI a question through the simplified validation surface (starts a validation job).

tools.name: claude_request
tools.description: Run a Claude Code CLI request synchronously (when async jobs are enabled, auto-defers to a pollable job past the sync deadline; otherwise runs to completion). Requires exactly one of prompt or promptParts.

prompt.description: Prompt text for Claude (mutually exclusive with promptParts)

promptParts.description: Cache-aware structured prompt: { system?, tools?, context?, task, cacheControl? }. Use for repeated calls that share a stable prefix — `system`/`tools`/`context` are the stable head; `task` is the volatile tail (never marked). Set `cacheControl: { system?: boolean, tools?: boolean, context?: boolean }` to opt into explicit Anthropic prefix caching via `--input-format stream-json` (slice κ). Requires `outputFormat: 'stream-json'` and hard-codes `ttl='1h'` (Anthropic rejects 5m blocks after Claude Code's 1h-marked session-wrap content). Mutually exclusive with `prompt`. The stable prefix hash is logged to the flight recorder for cache_state aggregates.

model.description: Model name or alias (e.g. sonnet, claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929, latest)

outputFormat.description: Output format (text|json|stream-json). DEFAULT: stream-json — the gateway parses NDJSON usage events to extract input/output/cache_read/cache_creation tokens + cost + model, persists them to the flight recorder for cache_state aggregates, and still returns the assistant text. Override to 'text' only when you truly want unparsed stdout (loses observability).

sessionId.description: On a fresh request this id is emitted as Claude --session-id <uuid> (must be a valid UUID that does not already exist). Resume the latest cwd conversation with continueSession:true. gw-* ids are not valid Claude --session-id values.

continueSession.description: Continue the most recent Claude conversation in this cwd (emits --continue; real CLI continuity).

dangerouslySkipPermissions.description: DEPRECATED: prefer `permissionMode: "bypassPermissions"`. Maps to it when `permissionMode` is unset.

permissionMode.description: Claude --permission-mode: default|acceptEdits|plan|auto|dontAsk|bypassPermissions. `default` is a no-op (no flag emitted).

agent.description: Claude --agent: dispatch to a named single sub-agent.

agents.description: Claude --agents: inline JSON map of agent name → { description, prompt, tools?, model? }.

forkSession.description: Claude --fork-session: branch from an existing session into a fresh fork.

systemPrompt.description: Claude --system-prompt: replace the system prompt entirely. Mutually exclusive with appendSystemPrompt.

appendSystemPrompt.description: Claude --append-system-prompt: append to the existing system prompt. Mutually exclusive with systemPrompt.

maxBudgetUsd.description: Claude --max-budget-usd: spend cap for this request in USD.

maxTurns.description: Claude --max-turns: cap on agent loop iterations.

excludeDynamicSystemPromptSections.description: Claude --exclude-dynamic-system-prompt-sections: trim dynamic context blocks from the system prompt.

fallbackModel.description: Claude --fallback-model: model name to auto-fallback to when the default model is overloaded (effective only with --print, which the gateway always uses).

jsonSchema.description: Claude --json-schema: JSON Schema literal (NOT a path) constraining structured output. Object values are JSON.stringify-d; string values are passed verbatim. Use with outputFormat='json'. Set outputFormat:json so the gateway treats the reply as structured output (skips response optimization and warning injection); the default output format is not json, so pass it explicitly.

addDir.description: Claude --add-dir: additional directories the CLI is allowed to read/write beyond the process cwd. Each entry is emitted as its own --add-dir instance. Stdio/local callers may pass local paths directly. Remote HTTP/OAuth callers must use relative paths inside a selected registered workspace. Do not call workspace_* tools to fix stdio/local provider path access.

noSessionPersistence.description: Claude --no-session-persistence: do not write this session to disk (ephemeral one-shot runs; mirrors codex --ephemeral).

settingSources.description: Claude --setting-sources: comma-separated setting sources to load (user|project|local) for reproducible/isolated headless runs.

settings.description: Claude --settings: path to a settings JSON file or a JSON literal of additional settings. Powerful: settings can define hooks/permissions/model; passed verbatim.

tools.description: Claude --tools: restrict the available built-in tool set (distinct from allowedTools permission gating). Pass [""] to disable all tools.

includeHookEvents.description: Claude --include-hook-events: include all hook lifecycle events in the output stream. Only takes effect with outputFormat=stream-json (the default).

replayUserMessages.description: Claude --replay-user-messages: re-emit user messages from stdin back on stdout for acknowledgment. Only works with input-format=stream-json and outputFormat=stream-json (the cacheControl path).

systemPromptFile.description: Claude --system-prompt-file: replace the system prompt from a file path (path variant of systemPrompt).

appendSystemPromptFile.description: Claude --append-system-prompt-file: append a system prompt from a file path (path variant of appendSystemPrompt).

name.description: Claude --name: display name for this session (shown in pickers/titles).

pluginDir.description: Claude --plugin-dir: load a plugin from a directory or .zip for this session only. One --plugin-dir instance per entry.

pluginUrl.description: Claude --plugin-url: load a plugin .zip from a URL for this session only. One --plugin-url instance per entry.

safeMode.description: Claude --safe-mode: start with all customizations (CLAUDE.md, skills, plugins, hooks, MCP, commands, agents) disabled for troubleshooting. Explicit opt-in.

bare.description: Claude --bare: minimal mode (skip hooks, LSP, plugin sync, attribution, auto-memory, keychain reads, CLAUDE.md auto-discovery). Explicit opt-in.

debug.description: Claude -d/--debug: enable debug mode. true emits a bare --debug; a string emits --debug <filter> (e.g. "api,hooks"). Debug output goes to stderr only.

debugFile.description: Claude --debug-file: write debug logs to a specific file path (enables debug mode).

workspace.description: Registered workspace alias for remote HTTP/OAuth provider calls. Do not use this field, workspace_list, or workspace_register_existing_repo as a fallback for stdio/local provider path access; pass workingDir/addDir/includeDirs directly instead.

worktree.description: Slice λ: run this request inside a dedicated git worktree owned by the gateway. `true` creates a fresh worktree at `<repoRoot>/.worktrees/<uuid>` branched from HEAD. `{ name?, ref? }` lets the caller supply a sanitized name and/or a git ref (default: HEAD). When the request carries a sessionId and the session already has a worktree, that worktree is reused. The gateway spawns the child CLI with `cwd: <worktree-path>` — no `-w`/`--worktree` flag is ever emitted to the underlying CLI. On session_delete or TTL eviction the gateway runs `git worktree remove --force`. Successful responses are prefixed with `[gateway] worktree=<absolute-path>\n` so callers can use the path. NOTE: callers should `.gitignore` the `.worktrees/` directory in their repo (the gateway does NOT auto-gitignore — see slice λ spec Q4).

approvalStrategy.description: Approval strategy: legacy (default) lets the provider CLI's own flags decide; mcp_managed routes the run through the gateway approval gate (required for approvalPolicy and approval_list). Under mcp_managed the caller's permissionMode/alwaysApprove may be overridden by the gate.

approvalPolicy.description: Approval policy when approvalStrategy is mcp_managed: strict|balanced|permissive (default balanced). Ignored under legacy strategy.

strictMcpConfig.description: Restrict Claude to provided MCP config only

compressResponse.description: Compress the response display text via the native compressor (default: [compression].enabled in config.toml, off unless opted in). Skipped for structured output.

idleTimeoutMs.description: Idle timeout in ms (min 30s, max 1h, omit=CLI default). Idle enforcement applies only when outputFormat is stream-json; it is ignored for text/json.

forceRefresh.description: Bypass dedup and force a fresh CLI run even if a recent identical request exists

tools.name: cli_upgrade
tools.description: Plan (dryRun, default true) or execute an upgrade for one provider CLI using its native update mechanism.

dryRun.description: When true, return the upgrade plan without running it

timeoutMs.description: Upgrade timeout in ms when dryRun=false

tools.name: cli_versions
tools.description: Report installed provider CLI versions, availability, and login status for all registered CLI providers (claude|codex|gemini|grok|mistral|devin|cursor) or one.

tools.name: codex_fork_session
tools.description: Fork an existing Codex session into a new branch (codex fork <ID|--last>) and run a prompt against the fork without mutating the original.

prompt.description: Prompt text for the forked Codex session

sessionId.description: Codex session UUID to fork from. Mutually exclusive with `forkLast`.

forkLast.description: Fork from the most recent Codex session. Mutually exclusive with `sessionId`.

model.description: Model name or alias (e.g. gpt-5.5, latest)

sandboxMode.description: Codex --sandbox. Omit = Codex exec built-in default (read-only; cannot write files). Pass workspace-write to let Codex edit files in the working dir, or danger-full-access for unrestricted access.

askForApproval.description: DEPRECATED compatibility input: accepted but ignored because current Codex no longer accepts --ask-for-approval.

sessionId.description: Codex session UUID to resume via `codex exec resume <ID>`. Must be a real Codex session ID (from `~/.codex/sessions/` or the `codex resume` picker). Gateway-generated `gw-*` IDs are rejected. For a brand-new session no resumable sessionId is returned; continue with resumeLatest:true or a real Codex UUID.

resumeLatest.description: Resume the most recent Codex session via `codex exec resume --last`. Ignored if sessionId is set. A brand-new session returns no resumable sessionId; continue with resumeLatest:true or a real Codex UUID.

compressResponse.description: Compress the response display text via the native compressor (default: [compression].enabled in config.toml, off unless opted in). Skipped for structured output.

idleTimeoutMs.description: Idle timeout in ms (min 30s, max 1h, omit=CLI default)

forceRefresh.description: Bypass dedup and force a fresh CLI run even if a recent identical request exists

outputFormat.description: Codex caller-facing output format. Token/cache usage is recorded in the flight recorder regardless. `text` (default) returns the plain reply; `json` returns the raw `--json` JSONL event stream.

outputSchema.description: Codex --output-schema. Pass a path (string) or an inline JSON Schema object; object is materialised to a 0o600 temp file under os.tmpdir() and deleted after the run.

search.description: DEPRECATED compatibility input: accepted but ignored because current Codex exec no longer accepts --search.

configOverrides.description: Codex -c key=value overrides. Keys: /^[a-zA-Z0-9._]+$/. Values: no CR/LF.

ephemeral.description: Codex --ephemeral: do not persist the session to disk.

images.description: Codex -i <path>: image attachments. Each path must exist; missing paths fail fast.

ignoreRules.description: Codex --ignore-rules: skip project rule files for this run.

workingDir.description: Codex -C/--cd <DIR>: working root for this session. Emitted on new sessions only; resume inherits the original session's cwd via CODEX_RESUME_FILTERED_FLAGS. Stdio/local callers may pass local paths directly. Remote HTTP/OAuth callers must use relative paths inside a selected registered workspace. Do not call workspace_* tools to fix stdio/local provider path access.

addDir.description: Codex --add-dir <DIR>: additional writable workspace directories. Emitted once per entry on new sessions only; resume inherits the original session's writable-dir policy. Stdio/local callers may pass local paths directly. Remote HTTP/OAuth callers must use relative paths inside a selected registered workspace. Do not call workspace_* tools to fix stdio/local provider path access.

enable.description: Codex --enable <FEATURE> (repeatable): enable a feature for this run (equivalent to -c features.<name>=true). Accepted on resume.

disable.description: Codex --disable <FEATURE> (repeatable): disable a feature for this run (equivalent to -c features.<name>=false). Accepted on resume.

strictConfig.description: Codex --strict-config: error out when config.toml contains fields not recognized by this Codex version. New sessions only.

oss.description: Codex --oss: use the open-source provider. New sessions only.

localProvider.description: Codex --local-provider: local OSS provider (lmstudio|ollama), used with oss. New sessions only.

color.description: Codex --color: output color mode (always|never|auto). New sessions only.

outputLastMessage.description: Codex -o/--output-last-message <FILE>: write the agent's last message to a file. New sessions only.

tools.name: cursor_request
tools.description: Run a Cursor Agent CLI request synchronously (auto-defers to a pollable job past the sync deadline when async jobs are enabled; otherwise runs to completion). Headless print mode (`cursor-agent --print`).

prompt.description: Prompt text for Cursor Agent CLI

model.description: Model name or alias passed via --model

mode.description: Cursor execution mode: plan (read-only planning) or ask (Q&A/read-only)

transport.description: Transport selector. Default `cli` uses cursor-agent --print. `acp` uses the native cursor-agent acp transport and fails closed unless [acp] and [acp.providers.cursor].runtime_enabled are true.

force.description: Emit --force (Cursor yolo mode; auto-allows commands unless explicitly denied)

autoReview.description: Emit --auto-review (Cursor Smart Auto classifier for tool calls)

trust.description: Trust the workspace in headless mode (--trust)

workspace.description: Workspace directory or saved workspace name (--workspace). Remote HTTP/OAuth callers must pass a registered workspace alias; local stdio callers may pass local Cursor workspace paths.

sessionId.description: Cursor chat/session ID to resume (emits --resume <id>). Note: the gw-* id minted for a brand-new gateway session is not resumable via sessionId; continue with resumeLatest:true.

resumeLatest.description: Resume the latest Cursor chat (--continue). Note: the gw-* id minted for a brand-new gateway session is not resumable via sessionId; continue with resumeLatest:true.

approvalStrategy.description: Approval strategy: legacy (default) lets Cursor's own flags decide; mcp_managed routes high-impact Cursor controls (force, trust, sandbox disabled) through the gateway approval gate.

approvalPolicy.description: Approval policy when approvalStrategy is mcp_managed: strict|balanced|permissive (default balanced). Ignored under legacy strategy.

compressResponse.description: Compress the response display text via the native compressor (default: [compression].enabled in config.toml, off unless opted in). Skipped for structured output.

idleTimeoutMs.description: Idle timeout in ms (min 30s, max 1h, omit=CLI default)

forceRefresh.description: Bypass dedup and force a fresh CLI run even if a recent identical request exists

tools.name: devin_request
tools.description: Run a Cognition Devin CLI request synchronously (auto-defers to a pollable job past the sync deadline when async jobs are enabled; otherwise runs to completion). Headless print mode (`devin -p`).

prompt.description: Prompt text for Devin CLI. Required in practice; promptFile is additive (loads an initial prompt from a file).

model.description: Model name or alias (e.g. opus, latest)

transport.description: Transport: 'cli' (default) runs the Devin CLI; 'acp' routes through `devin acp` when [acp].enabled and the provider's runtime_enabled are set (fails closed otherwise).

permissionMode.description: Devin CLI permission mode (--permission-mode). auto auto-approves read-only tools; smart additionally auto-runs actions a fast model judges safe; dangerous auto-approves all. When omitted, Devin uses its own headless default; pass auto or smart for unattended runs.

promptFile.description: Load the initial prompt from a file (--prompt-file)

sandbox.description: Run the Devin session in a sandbox (Devin --sandbox). Safety control: never defaulted on; pass true to opt in.

exportSession.description: Export the session (Devin --export [<PATH>]). true emits a bare --export; a string path emits --export <path>.

respectWorkspaceTrust.description: Respect workspace trust (Devin --respect-workspace-trust <bool>). Devin defaults true for interactive and false for print mode; set explicitly to override.

agentType.description: ACP agent variant for transport=acp (`devin acp --agent-type`): 'summarizer' (no tools, text summary) or 'review' (read-only + shell code-review). Ignored for the CLI transport.

sessionId.description: Devin session ID to resume (emits --resume <id>; use resumeLatest for --continue). Note: the gw-* id minted for a brand-new session is not resumable via sessionId; continue with resumeLatest:true.

resumeLatest.description: Resume the most recent Devin session in cwd (--continue). Note: the gw-* id minted for a brand-new session is not resumable via sessionId; continue with resumeLatest:true.

compressResponse.description: Compress the response display text via the native compressor (default: [compression].enabled in config.toml, off unless opted in). Skipped for structured output.

idleTimeoutMs.description: Idle timeout in ms (min 30s, max 1h, omit=CLI default)

forceRefresh.description: Bypass dedup and force a fresh CLI run even if a recent identical request exists

tools.name: gemini_request
tools.description: Run a Google Antigravity CLI (`agy`) request through the Gemini-compatible gateway tool synchronously (when async jobs are enabled, auto-defers to a pollable job past the sync deadline; otherwise runs to completion). Requires exactly one of prompt or promptParts.

prompt.description: Prompt text for Antigravity CLI (mutually exclusive with promptParts)

promptParts.description: Cache-aware structured prompt: { system?, tools?, context?, task }. Mutually exclusive with prompt. Stable parts hash into cache_state for prefix-discipline tracking.

model.description: Model name or alias passed to agy --model (e.g. gemini-3-pro-preview, gemini-2.5-flash, pro, flash, latest)

sessionId.description: Antigravity conversation ID to resume (emits --conversation <id>). agy owns conversation ids; a fresh request returns no resumable sessionId, continue via resumeLatest:true.

resumeLatest.description: Continue the most recent conversation. agy owns conversation ids; a fresh request returns no resumable sessionId, continue via resumeLatest:true.

approvalMode.description: Approval mode. Only default (prompted) and yolo (auto-approve all, emits --dangerously-skip-permissions) work with the Antigravity agy headless path; auto_edit and plan are rejected at request time (agy has no headless accept-edits/plan mode).

approvalStrategy.description: Approval strategy: legacy (default) lets the provider CLI's own flags decide; mcp_managed routes the run through the gateway approval gate (required for approvalPolicy and approval_list). Under mcp_managed the caller's permissionMode/alwaysApprove may be overridden by the gate.

approvalPolicy.description: Approval policy when approvalStrategy is mcp_managed: strict|balanced|permissive (default balanced). Ignored under legacy strategy.

mcpServers.description: MCP server names accepted for approval tracking only; Antigravity manages its own MCP configuration.

allowedTools.description: Unsupported for Antigravity CLI; non-empty values are rejected

includeDirs.description: Additional workspace directories passed as --add-dir. Stdio/local callers may pass local paths directly. Remote HTTP/OAuth callers must use relative paths inside a selected registered workspace. Do not call workspace_* tools to fix stdio/local provider path access.

compressResponse.description: Compress the response display text via the native compressor (default: [compression].enabled in config.toml, off unless opted in). Skipped for structured output.

idleTimeoutMs.description: Idle timeout in ms (min 30s, max 1h, omit=CLI default)

forceRefresh.description: Bypass dedup and force a fresh CLI run even if a recent identical request exists

outputFormat.description: Output format. The Antigravity agy headless path emits text only; json and stream-json are rejected at request time. Per-request token usage and cost are therefore not available for gemini.

policyFiles.description: Unsupported for Antigravity CLI; non-empty values are rejected.

adminPolicyFiles.description: Unsupported for Antigravity CLI; non-empty values are rejected.

attachments.description: Unsupported for Antigravity CLI; non-empty values are rejected.

skipTrust.description: Unsupported for Antigravity CLI; true is rejected.

yolo.description: Emit `--dangerously-skip-permissions` to auto-approve all actions. Routed through the same approval gate. Under mcp_managed the gate still decides.

project.description: Antigravity 1.0.13 --project <ID>: select the project for this session. Mutually exclusive with newProject.

newProject.description: Antigravity 1.0.13 --new-project: create a new project for this session. Mutually exclusive with project.

printTimeout.description: Antigravity --print-timeout <DURATION>: print-mode wait timeout as a Go duration string (e.g. '5m0s', '30s').

workspace.description: Registered workspace alias for remote HTTP/OAuth provider calls. Do not use this field, workspace_list, or workspace_register_existing_repo as a fallback for stdio/local provider path access; pass workingDir/addDir/includeDirs directly instead.

worktree.description: Slice λ: run this request inside a dedicated git worktree owned by the gateway. `true` creates a fresh worktree at `<repoRoot>/.worktrees/<uuid>` branched from HEAD. `{ name?, ref? }` lets the caller supply a sanitized name and/or a git ref (default: HEAD). When the request carries a sessionId and the session already has a worktree, that worktree is reused. The gateway spawns the child CLI with `cwd: <worktree-path>` — no `-w`/`--worktree` flag is ever emitted to the underlying CLI. On session_delete or TTL eviction the gateway runs `git worktree remove --force`. Successful responses are prefixed with `[gateway] worktree=<absolute-path>\n` so callers can use the path. NOTE: callers should `.gitignore` the `.worktrees/` directory in their repo (the gateway does NOT auto-gitignore — see slice λ spec Q4).

tools.name: grok_request
tools.description: Run an xAI Grok CLI request synchronously (when async jobs are enabled, auto-defers to a pollable job past the sync deadline; otherwise runs to completion). Requires exactly one of prompt or promptParts.

prompt.description: Prompt text for Grok (mutually exclusive with promptParts)

promptParts.description: Cache-aware structured prompt: { system?, tools?, context?, task }. Mutually exclusive with prompt. Stable parts hash into cache_state for prefix-discipline tracking.

model.description: Model name or alias (e.g. grok-build, latest)

transport.description: Transport: 'cli' (default) runs the Grok CLI; 'acp' routes through `grok agent stdio` when [acp].enabled and the provider's runtime_enabled are set (fails closed otherwise).

outputFormat.description: Output format (plain|json|streaming-json). Grok default is plain.

allowedTools.description: Allowed built-in tools (passed as --tools comma list)

disallowedTools.description: Disallowed built-in tools (passed as --disallowed-tools comma list)

maxTurns.description: Grok `--max-turns N`: cap on agent-loop iterations for cost / latency control (Phase 4 slice δ). Bounded to safe integers ≤ 10000.

workingDir.description: Grok --cwd <DIR>: working directory for this invocation. Lets headless callers run Grok against a directory other than the gateway process's cwd.

sandbox.description: Grok --sandbox <PROFILE>: sandbox profile for filesystem and network access. Freeform per `grok --help` (no enum constraint on Grok 0.1.210); also settable via GROK_SANDBOX env var. Caller responsibility to pass a valid profile name.

rules.description: Grok --rules <RULES>: extra rules to append to the system prompt. Supports `@file` prefix per `grok --help` to load from a file; gateway passes the value verbatim and lets Grok parse the prefix.

systemPromptOverride.description: Grok --system-prompt-override <PROMPT>: replace the agent's system prompt entirely. Distinct from Claude's --system-prompt / --append-system-prompt (Grok has only one override flag, not a pair).

allow.description: Grok --allow <RULE>: permission allow rules. Each entry is emitted as its own --allow instance (per `grok --help`: "Repeat to add multiple rules").

deny.description: Grok --deny <RULE>: permission deny rules. Each entry is emitted as its own --deny instance (per `grok --help`: "Repeat to add multiple rules").

compactionMode.description: Grok --compaction-mode: summary (default; no pointer) | transcript (points at the raw transcript) | segments (persists per-segment markdown to grep). Sets GROK_COMPACTION_MODE.

compactionDetail.description: Grok --compaction-detail: verbatim segment detail (none|minimal|balanced|verbose, default verbose). Only affects `--compaction-mode segments`. Sets GROK_COMPACTION_DETAIL.

agent.description: Grok --agent <NAME>: agent name or definition file path.

bestOfN.description: Grok --best-of-n <N>: run the task N ways in parallel and pick the best (headless only).

check.description: Grok --check: append a self-verification loop to the prompt (headless only).

disableWebSearch.description: Grok --disable-web-search: disable web search and remote retrieval tools.

todoGate.description: Grok --todo-gate: enable runtime turn-end TodoGate for this session (session-scoped, not persisted).

verbatim.description: Grok --verbatim: send the prompt exactly as given. Also skips gateway optimizePrompt when true.

promptFile.description: Grok --prompt-file <PATH>: single-turn prompt loaded from a file.

single.description: Grok --single <PROMPT>: single-turn prompt (in addition to gateway -p).

noAltScreen.description: Grok --no-alt-screen: run inline without alt screen.

restoreCode.description: Grok --restore-code: check out the original session commit when resuming.

leaderSocket.description: Grok 0.2.32+ --leader-socket <PATH>: custom leader socket path (default ~/.grok/leader.sock). Targets an isolated leader process, e.g. a local/branch Grok build; name it ~/.grok/leader-*.sock to keep `grok leader list/kill` discovery working.

sessionId.description: Provider-native session ID to resume (emits --resume <id>; use resumeLatest for --continue). Note: the gw-* id minted for a brand-new session is not resumable via sessionId; continue with resumeLatest:true.

resumeLatest.description: Resume most recent Grok session in cwd (--continue). Note: the gw-* id minted for a brand-new session is not resumable via sessionId; continue with resumeLatest:true.

approvalStrategy.description: Approval strategy: legacy (default) lets the provider CLI's own flags decide; mcp_managed routes the run through the gateway approval gate (required for approvalPolicy and approval_list). Under mcp_managed the caller's permissionMode/alwaysApprove may be overridden by the gate.

approvalPolicy.description: Approval policy when approvalStrategy is mcp_managed: strict|balanced|permissive (default balanced). Ignored under legacy strategy.

mcpServers.description: MCP server names for approval tracking (Grok manages its own MCP config via `grok mcp`)

compressResponse.description: Compress the response display text via the native compressor (default: [compression].enabled in config.toml, off unless opted in). Skipped for structured output.

idleTimeoutMs.description: Idle timeout in ms (min 30s, max 1h). Omit = gateway default of 600000ms (10 min) with no output before the process is killed.

forceRefresh.description: Bypass dedup and force a fresh CLI run even if a recent identical request exists

agents.description: Grok --agents <JSON>: inline subagent definitions (JSON string or name → { description, prompt, … } map).

promptJson.description: Grok --prompt-json <JSON>: single-turn prompt JSON blocks (string or serializable value).

nativeWorktree.description: Grok -w/--worktree: native CLI worktree flag (`true` → bare `--worktree`, string → named). NOT gateway slice λ `worktree`.

worktreeRef.description: Grok 0.2.73 --worktree-ref <REF>: git ref (branch/tag/commit) to base the native worktree on. Requires nativeWorktree.

forkSession.description: Grok 0.2.73 --fork-session: when resuming (--resume/--continue), fork into a new session ID instead of reusing the original.

jsonSchema.description: Grok 0.2.73 --json-schema: constrain output to a JSON Schema (string literal or object; implies json output). Set outputFormat:json so the gateway treats the reply as structured output (skips response optimization and warning injection); the default output format is not json, so pass it explicitly.

workspace.description: Registered workspace alias for remote HTTP/OAuth provider calls. Do not use this field, workspace_list, or workspace_register_existing_repo as a fallback for stdio/local provider path access; pass workingDir/addDir/includeDirs directly instead.

worktree.description: Slice λ: run this request inside a dedicated git worktree owned by the gateway. `true` creates a fresh worktree at `<repoRoot>/.worktrees/<uuid>` branched from HEAD. `{ name?, ref? }` lets the caller supply a sanitized name and/or a git ref (default: HEAD). When the request carries a sessionId and the session already has a worktree, that worktree is reused. The gateway spawns the child CLI with `cwd: <worktree-path>` — no `-w`/`--worktree` flag is ever emitted to the underlying CLI. On session_delete or TTL eviction the gateway runs `git worktree remove --force`. Successful responses are prefixed with `[gateway] worktree=<absolute-path>\n` so callers can use the path. NOTE: callers should `.gitignore` the `.worktrees/` directory in their repo (the gateway does NOT auto-gitignore — see slice λ spec Q4).

worktreeRef.description: Grok 0.2.73 --worktree-ref <REF>: git ref (branch/tag/commit) to base the native worktree on. Requires nativeWorktree.

forkSession.description: Grok 0.2.73 --fork-session: when resuming (--resume/--continue), fork into a new session ID instead of reusing the original.

jsonSchema.description: Grok 0.2.73 --json-schema: constrain output to a JSON Schema (string literal or object; implies json output). Set outputFormat:json so the gateway treats the reply as structured output (skips response optimization and warning injection); the default output format is not json, so pass it explicitly.

workspace.description: Registered workspace alias for remote HTTP/OAuth provider calls. Do not use this field, workspace_list, or workspace_register_existing_repo as a fallback for stdio/local provider path access; pass workingDir/addDir/includeDirs directly instead.

worktree.description: Slice λ: run this request inside a dedicated git worktree owned by the gateway. `true` creates a fresh worktree at `<repoRoot>/.worktrees/<uuid>` branched from HEAD. `{ name?, ref? }` lets the caller supply a sanitized name and/or a git ref (default: HEAD). When the request carries a sessionId and the session already has a worktree, that worktree is reused. The gateway spawns the child CLI with `cwd: <worktree-path>` — no `-w`/`--worktree` flag is ever emitted to the underlying CLI. On session_delete or TTL eviction the gateway runs `git worktree remove --force`. Successful responses are prefixed with `[gateway] worktree=<absolute-path>\n` so callers can use the path. NOTE: callers should `.gitignore` the `.worktrees/` directory in their repo (the gateway does NOT auto-gitignore — see slice λ spec Q4).

tools.name: job_result
tools.description: Collect a VALIDATION job's normalized provider output — distinct from llm_job_result, which returns raw provider request job output.

provider.description: Provider that produced the job, used for normalized validation output.

tools.name: job_status
tools.description: Check a VALIDATION job's status (jobs started by validate_with_models/ask_model/etc.) — distinct from llm_job_status, which tracks provider request jobs.

tools.name: list_available_models
tools.description: List models and capabilities for every available provider CLI (takes no arguments; complements per-provider list_models).

tools.name: list_models
tools.description: List models, aliases, and defaults for one provider (claude|codex|gemini|grok|mistral|devin|cursor, or an enabled API provider name), or omit cli to list all providers. API providers are returned under an `apiProviders` array.

cli.description: Provider filter (claude|codex|gemini|grok|mistral|devin|cursor, or an enabled API provider name)

tools.name: llm_job_cancel
tools.description: Cancel a running gateway async or deferred-sync job by jobId.

tools.name: llm_job_result
tools.description: Retrieve captured stdout/stderr for a gateway async or deferred-sync job by jobId.

tools.name: llm_job_status
tools.description: Check lifecycle status (queued|running|completed|failed|canceled|orphaned) of a gateway async or deferred-sync job by jobId.

tools.name: llm_process_health
tools.description: Report gateway process health: async-job manager state plus the resolved persistence configuration and paths.

tools.name: llm_request_result
tools.description: Read back any persisted request (sync or async) from the flight recorder by correlationId, including prompt and response.

correlationId.description: Correlation id from a prior request's structuredContent.correlationId (sync or async)

maxChars.description: Max chars of the persisted response to return

includePrompt.description: Include the full persisted prompt text in the result

tools.name: mistral_request
tools.description: Run a Mistral Vibe CLI request synchronously (when async jobs are enabled, auto-defers to a pollable job past the sync deadline; otherwise runs to completion). Requires exactly one of prompt or promptParts. Defaults to --agent accept-edits (auto-accepts file edits; dangerous ops such as shell stay gated); pass permissionMode auto-approve (or set LLM_GATEWAY_APPROVAL_ALLOW_BYPASS) for unattended tool execution.

prompt.description: Prompt text for Mistral Vibe (mutually exclusive with promptParts)

promptParts.description: Cache-aware structured prompt: { system?, tools?, context?, task }. Mutually exclusive with prompt. Stable parts hash into cache_state for prefix-discipline tracking.

model.description: Model alias (e.g. mistral-medium-3.5, latest). Resolved alias is injected via VIBE_ACTIVE_MODEL env var; Vibe has no --model flag.

transport.description: Transport: 'cli' (default) runs the Vibe CLI; 'acp' routes through `vibe-acp` when [acp].enabled and the provider's runtime_enabled are set (fails closed otherwise).

outputFormat.description: Output format for Vibe 2.x (text|json|streaming). Legacy aliases plain→text and stream-json→streaming are accepted.

sessionId.description: Session ID (user-provided CLI handle for --resume). Current Vibe defaults session logging on; doctor flags explicit [session_logging] enabled = false. Note: the gw-* id minted for a brand-new session is not resumable via sessionId; continue with resumeLatest:true.

resumeLatest.description: Resume most recent Vibe session in cwd (--continue). Note: the gw-* id minted for a brand-new session is not resumable via sessionId; continue with resumeLatest:true.

permissionMode.description: Vibe --agent name. Builtins: default|plan|accept-edits|auto-approve; Vibe also accepts install-gated builtins (e.g. lean) and custom agents from ~/.vibe/agents, so any name is passed through. Defaults to accept-edits; pass auto-approve (or set LLM_GATEWAY_APPROVAL_ALLOW_BYPASS) for unattended tool execution.

approvalStrategy.description: Approval strategy: legacy (default) lets the provider CLI's own flags decide; mcp_managed routes the run through the gateway approval gate (required for approvalPolicy and approval_list). Under mcp_managed the caller's permissionMode/alwaysApprove may be overridden by the gate.

approvalPolicy.description: Approval policy when approvalStrategy is mcp_managed: strict|balanced|permissive (default balanced). Ignored under legacy strategy.

mcpServers.description: MCP server names for approval tracking (Vibe manages its own MCP config via `vibe mcp`)

allowedTools.description: Allowlist of built-in tools — each emitted as a separate --enabled-tools <tool> flag

disallowedTools.description: Accepted for caller parity; Vibe has no deny-list flag, so values are ignored (a warning is logged).

compressResponse.description: Compress the response display text via the native compressor (default: [compression].enabled in config.toml, off unless opted in). Skipped for structured output.

idleTimeoutMs.description: Idle timeout in ms (min 30s, max 1h, omit=CLI default)

forceRefresh.description: Bypass dedup and force a fresh CLI run even if a recent identical request exists

trust.description: Emit `--trust` so Vibe trusts the cwd for this invocation only (not persisted to trusted_folders.toml) and skips the interactive trust prompt (Phase 4 slice γ).

maxTurns.description: Vibe `--max-turns N`: cap the agent-loop iteration count (programmatic mode only, Phase 4 slice δ). Bounded to safe integers ≤ 10000.

maxPrice.description: Vibe `--max-price DOLLARS`: interrupt the session when cumulative cost crosses this cap (programmatic mode only, Phase 4 slice δ). Bounded to finite values ≤ 10000 USD.

maxTokens.description: Vibe `--max-tokens N`: cap cumulative prompt + completion tokens for the session (programmatic mode only). Bounded to safe integers ≤ 100000000.

workingDir.description: Vibe --workdir <DIR>: change to this directory before running. Single value (Vibe accepts one --workdir per invocation). Stdio/local callers may pass local paths directly. Remote HTTP/OAuth callers must use relative paths inside a selected registered workspace. Do not call workspace_* tools to fix stdio/local provider path access.

addDir.description: Vibe --add-dir <DIR>: additional writable workspace directories. Each entry is emitted as its own --add-dir instance (Vibe states this flag may be specified multiple times). Stdio/local callers may pass local paths directly. Remote HTTP/OAuth callers must use relative paths inside a selected registered workspace. Do not call workspace_* tools to fix stdio/local provider path access.

workspace.description: Registered workspace alias for remote HTTP/OAuth provider calls. Do not use this field, workspace_list, or workspace_register_existing_repo as a fallback for stdio/local provider path access; pass workingDir/addDir/includeDirs directly instead.

worktree.description: Slice λ: run this request inside a dedicated git worktree owned by the gateway. `true` creates a fresh worktree at `<repoRoot>/.worktrees/<uuid>` branched from HEAD. `{ name?, ref? }` lets the caller supply a sanitized name and/or a git ref (default: HEAD). When the request carries a sessionId and the session already has a worktree, that worktree is reused. The gateway spawns the child CLI with `cwd: <worktree-path>` — no `-w`/`--worktree` flag is ever emitted to the underlying CLI. On session_delete or TTL eviction the gateway runs `git worktree remove --force`. Successful responses are prefixed with `[gateway] worktree=<absolute-path>\n` so callers can use the path. NOTE: callers should `.gitignore` the `.worktrees/` directory in their repo (the gateway does NOT auto-gitignore — see slice λ spec Q4).

tools.name: provider_admin_list
tools.description: List provider CLI admin operations (auth status, model list, mcp list, plugin list, doctor, etc.) available on the installed CLIs, projected from runtime discovery. Read-only.

includeUnavailable.description: Include operations the installed CLI does not advertise or the policy hides

tools.name: provider_admin_mutate
tools.description: Execute a MUTATING provider CLI admin operation (mcp add/remove, login/logout, plugin install/remove, session delete/archive, ...). Disabled unless [admin] allow_mutating_cli_admin_ops=true; routed through approval and audited.

provider.description: Provider whose admin operation to run

operationId.description: Mutating operation id from provider_admin_list, e.g. 'mcp.remove'

tools.name: provider_admin_run
tools.description: Execute a READ-ONLY provider CLI admin operation (from provider_admin_list) and return redacted output. Rejects mutating operations.

provider.description: Provider whose admin operation to run

operationId.description: Operation id from provider_admin_list, e.g. 'mcp.list' or 'doctor'

tools.name: provider_subcommand_contract
tools.description: Return the detailed read-only contract for exactly one declared provider CLI subcommand.

tools.name: provider_subcommand_drift
tools.description: Probe declared provider subcommand --help surfaces and return compact drift rows without raw help output.

includeClean.description: When false, return only unavailable or drifted command paths

tools.name: provider_subcommands_list
tools.description: Return a compact, filterable read-only catalog of declared provider CLI subcommands without flags or raw help.

commandPathPrefix.description: Optional command path prefix filter, e.g. ['agent']

tools.name: provider_tool_capabilities
tools.description: Report provider tool/feature capabilities and discovered local skill/tool integrations for claude|codex|gemini|grok|mistral|devin|cursor|grok_api, or an enabled API provider name.

cli.description: Provider filter (claude|codex|gemini|grok|mistral|devin|cursor|grok_api, or an enabled API provider name)

includeSkills.description: Include bounded local skill discovery results

includeProviderTools.description: Include provider-native tools extracted from local skills

includePaths.description: Include raw local filesystem paths in discovery output

tools.name: red_team_review
tools.description: Challenge a plan, answer, or document for risks and failure modes via provider CLIs (starts validation jobs).

content.description: Plan, answer, or document to challenge.

models.description: Providers to ask for adversarial review.

tools.name: second_opinion
tools.description: Ask one provider CLI to review an answer (starts a validation job; poll job_status, collect job_result).

model.description: Provider to ask for the second opinion.

tools.name: session_clear_all
tools.description: Delete all gateway session records, optionally scoped to one provider.

tools.name: session_create
tools.description: Create a gateway session record for a provider. NOTE: this is gateway bookkeeping (a plain UUID), not a provider-native session; Codex resume needs a real Codex UUID.

tools.name: session_delete
tools.description: Delete a gateway session record by ID (also removes any gateway-owned worktree attached to it).

tools.name: session_get
tools.description: Get one gateway session record by session ID, including recent request history when available.

tools.name: session_list
tools.description: List gateway session records and the active session per provider, optionally filtered by provider.

tools.name: session_set_active
tools.description: Set or clear the active session for a provider; the active session is used when a request omits sessionId.

tools.name: synthesize_validation
tools.description: Run an explicit judge model over already-collected validation results to produce a synthesis.

providerResults.description: Terminal normalized provider results from job_result.

judgeModel.description: Provider to run the judge synthesis.

validationId.description: Optional run id (from the kickoff response) to link this judge job back into the durable validation run.

tools.name: upstream_contracts
tools.description: Return the gateway's declared provider CLI contracts; with probeInstalled true, diff against installed --help surfaces to detect flag drift.

probeInstalled.description: When true, run local --help probes and compare advertised flags against the declared contract. Strongly recommended after any provider CLI upgrade to detect drift.

tools.name: validate_with_models
tools.description: Ask two or more provider CLIs to independently validate a question. Starts validation jobs — poll with job_status, collect with job_result (not llm_job_*).

models.description: Providers to ask. Defaults to Claude and Codex.

focus.default: correctness, missing assumptions, and practical next steps
focus.description: What reviewers should pay attention to.

judgeModel.description: Optional provider to run an explicit judge synthesis job.

tools.name: workspace_create
tools.description: Create a remote HTTP/OAuth workspace alias by creating a new local folder or git repo under a configured allowed root. Not for stdio/local provider path access. Requires LLM_GATEWAY_WORKSPACE_ADMIN=1 and OAuth scope workspace:admin.

alias.description: Registered workspace alias for remote HTTP/OAuth provider calls. Stdio/local callers should not use workspace_* tools to fix provider path access; pass local workingDir/addDir/includeDirs directly.

slug.description: Safe relative path under the allowed root.

tools.name: workspace_get
tools.description: Inspect a registered remote HTTP/OAuth workspace alias. Does not list files. Not needed for stdio/local provider calls; do not use workspace_* tools to fix local path access.

alias.description: Registered workspace alias for remote HTTP/OAuth provider calls. Stdio/local callers should not use workspace_* tools to fix provider path access; pass local workingDir/addDir/includeDirs directly.

tools.name: workspace_list
tools.description: List registered workspace aliases for remote HTTP/OAuth provider calls. Does not browse files. Stdio/local callers should not use workspace_* tools to fix provider path access; pass local workingDir/addDir/includeDirs directly.

tools.name: workspace_register_existing_repo
tools.description: Register an existing local Git repo as a remote HTTP/OAuth workspace alias. Not for stdio/local provider path access. Requires LLM_GATEWAY_WORKSPACE_ADMIN=1 and OAuth scope workspace:admin.

alias.description: Registered workspace alias for remote HTTP/OAuth provider calls. Stdio/local callers should not use workspace_* tools to fix provider path access; pass local workingDir/addDir/includeDirs directly.

path.description: Absolute path to an existing Git repo under an allowed root.
